pattern: The total cars every mechanic can finish by time t only grows as t grows, so binary search the answer instead of simulating who repairs what.
hints: For a fixed amount of time t, a mechanic of rank r can finish exactly floor(sqrt(t / r)) cars -- that count never decreases as t grows.
Sum that count across every mechanic to get a feasibility check: can everyone together finish at least `cars` cars by time t?
Binary search t between 1 and the time it would take the single fastest-ranked mechanic to do every car alone, using the feasibility check to shrink the range.

edge_inputs.description: single mechanic at the slowest rank with many cars
many mechanics all at the fastest rank
identical ranks, work should split evenly rather than favor one index
one very slow mechanic among fast ones should end up doing the least work

stress.description: many mechanics with random ranks and a large car count
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    ranks = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    cars = rng.randint(1, 1000000)
    return [ranks, cars]

oracle_python:
import heapq
class Solution:
    def repairCars(self, ranks, cars):
        heap = [(r, r, 1) for r in ranks]
        heapq.heapify(heap)
        served = 0
        last_time = 0
        while served < cars:
            finish, r, cnt = heapq.heappop(heap)
            served += 1
            last_time = finish
            heapq.heappush(heap, (r * (cnt + 1) * (cnt + 1), r, cnt + 1))
        return last_time
